text:
Centre for Epilepsy

Neurosciences Division 

Clinic Date: 14 May 2020 

Dr Asha Rahman  
Centre for Epilepsy  
Queen Square, London WC1N 3BG  

Dear Dr Asha Rahman,                                                                                                         

Luca Moretti, DOB: 22-11-1992, Hospital No: Q317562 NHS No. 9843216750  
Flat 3, 27 Hanway Street, London, W1T 1UF  

Epilepsy 

Medication: No regular antiseizure medication currently. Uses buccal midazolam as rescue per workplace protocol; not yet required.

Lamotrigine Blood Level: 

Not applicable (not prescribed).

Seizures: His initial event was in January 2020 in Germany, arising from sleep. He awoke with jerking of the left arm and facial twitching. A second event occurred in Italy the following May 2020, once more during the night, lasting four minutes with similar semiology.

I reviewed Luca Moretti today via telephone following referral from occupational health. He works as a commercial kitchen line cook in a high‑heat, fast‑paced setting, where he is frequently at the grill and fryer station. He reports both events occurred while travelling for culinary pop‑up work, with no daytime events and no witnessed loss of awareness at work. He denies antecedent fever, head injury, alcohol binge, or stimulant use. He drinks one coffee per shift and minimal alcohol. Sleep during those trips was reduced to 4–5 hours due to late service and early prep; he has since adjusted his routine to target 7 hours. There have been no further episodes since the second event. 

Interim safety and work context: The restaurant has implemented a buddy‑system for opening hot oil vats and a protocol to step away from the line if unwell. He reports no episodes of aura, twitching, or nocturnal confusion since May 2020. No tongue‑bite or incontinence reported with either event. Post‑event fatigue lasted several hours after the second episode. 

Investigations: Workplace GP arranged basic bloods (FBC, U&E, LFT, glucose, magnesium) in May 2020—patient reports all within normal limits; copies requested. We will arrange MRI brain with epilepsy protocol and an EEG including sleep deprivation to evaluate propensity for further events. 

Plan: 
- Proceed with MRI brain (epilepsy protocol) and routine EEG with sleep deprivation.  
- No start of regular antiseizure medication at present given two nocturnal events only and no recurrence since May 2020; will revisit pending investigations and any further events.  
- Provide written seizure first‑aid and return‑to‑work guidance for a hot‑kitchen environment (patient‑reported adherence already good).  
- Advise strict sleep regularity, avoidance of excess alcohol, and prompt reporting of any further events or daytime symptoms.  
- Follow‑up in clinic after investigations or sooner if recurrent events occur.

Yours sincerely

His initial event was in January 2020 in Germany, arising from sleep. He awoke with jerking of the left arm and facial twitching. A second event occurred in Italy the following May 2020, once more during the night, lasting four minutes with similar semiology

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter documents exactly two seizure events: one in January 2020 and a second in May 2020. It also states there have been no further episodes since the second event. Clinic date is 14 May 2020, and the second event occurred in May 2020; therefore, the patient has not been seizure free for at least 6 months as of the clinic date, so we cannot label as seizure free. The most concrete frequency we can derive around the relevant recent period is 2 events over approximately 4 months (January to May 2020). Thus, normalize to '2 per 4 month'.
